title: वास्तु पुरुष स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वास्तु पुरुष स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
वन्दे वास्तुपुरुषं सर्ववास्तुनिवासिनम्।
सर्वसौख्यप्रदं देवं सर्वापद्विनिवारकम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
नमस्ते वास्तुपुरुष भूशय्या विभूषित।
महाबलाय शान्ताय गृहक्षेत्रप्रदाय च॥ १॥

ब्रह्मा विष्णुश्च रुद्रश्च दिक्पालाश्चाखिला ग्रहाः।
त्वय्येव निवसन्त्यत्र वास्तुपूजितदेवता॥ २॥

ईशाने त्वं शिरः शम्भोः पूर्वे मुखमुदीरितम्।
आग्नेये दक्षिणो हस्तो नैऋत्ये चरणं तव॥ ३॥

वायव्ये वामहस्तस्ते हृदये ब्रह्मणो गृहम्।
नाभौ विष्णुः स्थितो नित्यं सर्वाङ्गे देवतागणः॥ ४॥

पद्मासनस्थो भगवान् वास्तुचक्रे विराजते।
ब्रह्माण्डगर्भे यो वास्तुः स नः पातु सदा शिवः॥ ५॥

चतुरशीतिपदे वास्तौ नित्यं सन्निहितो भव।
देवदानवगन्धर्वाः पिशाचा राक्षसास्तथा॥ ६॥

त्वत्प्रसादाद्विनश्यन्ति सर्वे वास्तुगता ग्रहाः।
सर्ववास्तुगतान् दोषान् त्वं निवारय सर्वदा॥ ७॥

वास्तुपुरुष सर्वज्ञ सर्वलोकहिताय च।
गृहारम्भे गृहस्थाने सदा सन्निहितो भव॥ ८॥

॥ अष्टदिक्पाल नमन ॥
ॐ इन्द्राय नमः — पूर्व दिशा।
ॐ अग्नये नमः — आग्नेय कोण।
ॐ यमाय नमः — दक्षिण दिशा।
ॐ निऋतये नमः — नैऋत्य कोण।
ॐ वरुणाय नमः — पश्चिम दिशा।
ॐ वायवे नमः — वायव्य कोण।
ॐ कुबेराय नमः — उत्तर दिशा।
ॐ ईशानाय नमः — ईशान कोण।

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
वास्तुपुरुषस्तोत्रमिदं यः पठेत्प्रयतः सदा।
वास्तुदोषो न जायेत गृहे सौख्यं प्रवर्धते॥

गृहप्रवेशे भूमिपूजने गृहारम्भे विशेषतः।
पठनीयं प्रयत्नेन वास्तुशान्तिकरं परम्॥

॥ इति श्री वास्तु पुरुष स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vastu Purusha Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vande Vastu-purusham Sarva-vastu-nivasinam.
Sarva-saukhya-pradam Devam Sarva-apad-vinivarakam.

|| Stotram ||
Namaste Vastu-purusha Bhushayya Vibhushita.
Mahabalaya Shantaya Griha-kshetra-pradaya Cha. || 1 ||

Brahma Vishnush-cha Rudrash-cha Dikpalash-chakhila Grahah.
Tvayy-eva Nivasanty-atra Vastu-pujita-devata. || 2 ||

Ishane Tvam Shirah Shambhoh Purve Mukham-udiritam.
Aagneye Dakshino Hasto Nairitye Charanam Tava. || 3 ||

Vayavye Vama-hastas-te Hridaye Brahmano Griham.
Nabhau Vishnuh Sthito Nityam Sarvange Devata-ganah. || 4 ||

Padmasana-stho Bhagavan Vastu-chakre Virajate.
Brahmanada-garbhe Yo Vastuh Sa Nah Patu Sada Shivah. || 5 ||

Chaturashiti-pade Vastau Nityam Sannihito Bhava.
Deva-danava-gandharvah Pishacha Rakshasas-tatha. || 6 ||

Tvat-prasadad-vinashyanti Sarve Vastu-gata Grahah.
Sarva-vastu-gatan Doshan Tvam Nivaraya Sarvada. || 7 ||

Vastu-purusha Sarvajna Sarva-loka-hitaya Cha.
Griha-arambhe Griha-sthane Sada Sannihito Bhava. || 8 ||

|| Ashta-dikpala Namana ||
Om Indraya Namah — Purva Disha.
Om Agnaye Namah — Aagneya Kona.
Om Yamaya Namah — Dakshina Disha.
Om Niritaye Namah — Nairitya Kona.
Om Varunaya Namah — Pashchima Disha.
Om Vayave Namah — Vayavya Kona.
Om Kuberaya Namah — Uttara Disha.
Om Ishanaya Namah — Ishana Kona.

|| Phalashruti ||
Vastu-purusha-stotram-idam Yah Pathet-prayatah Sada.
Vastu-dosho Na Jayeta Grihe Saukhyam Pravardhate.

Griha-praveshe Bhumi-pujane Griha-arambhe Visheshatah.
Pathaniyam Prayatnena Vastu-shanti-karam Param.

|| Iti Shri Vastu Purusha Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वास्तु पुरुष स्तोत्र में भूमि पर अधोमुख शयन करने वाले वास्तु पुरुष की स्तुति है। वास्तु शास्त्र के अनुसार प्रत्येक भवन/भूमि में वास्तु पुरुष निवास करते हैं और उनके शरीर के विभिन्न अंगों पर विभिन्न देवताओं का स्थान होता है।

वास्तु पुरुष के शरीर में ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, रुद्र, दिक्पाल और समस्त ग्रह निवास करते हैं। ईशान कोण में उनका शिर (शिव), पूर्व में मुख, आग्नेय में दाहिना हाथ, नैऋत्य में चरण, वायव्य में वामहस्त, हृदय में ब्रह्मा और नाभि में विष्णु स्थित हैं।

चौरासी पदों वाले वास्तुमण्डल में वे नित्य विराजमान हैं। उनकी कृपा से सभी वास्तुगत ग्रह और दोष नष्ट हो जाते हैं।

गृहप्रवेश, भूमिपूजन और गृहारम्भ के अवसर पर इस स्तोत्र का पाठ विशेष रूप से करना चाहिए।
benefits: Worships the Vastu Purusha who resides in every building and plot
Describes the cosmic body of Vastu Purusha with deities at each position
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of vastu-related doshas from homes and buildings
Invokes all eight directional guardians for complete protection
Essential recitation during Bhumi Pujan and Griha Pravesh ceremonies
Increases happiness and well-being in the household
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Vastu Purusha Stotram (वास्तु पुरुष स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Matsya Purana, Vastu Shastra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to increase happiness and well-being in the household as well as worship the Vastu Purusha who resides in every building and plot. Additionally, it is known to invoke all eight directional guardians for complete protection. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vastu Purusha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Vastu Purusha Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer for Vastu dosha removal with Ashta Dikpala Namana for home protection.
relatedSearches: Vastu Purusha Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit
Om Vastu Purushaya Namah mantra 108 times
Vastu Purusha story origin Shiva Andhakasura
Vastu Shanti puja vidhi mantra recitation

seoFaqs.question: Who is the Vastu Purusha and how did he come into being?
seoFaqs.answer: Vastu Purusha is the presiding deity of every plot of land and every constructed dwelling in Hindu architectural tradition. The Matsya Purana and Brihat Samhita relate that he was born from the sweat of Lord Shiva during the killing of the demon Andhakasura; the giant being grew uncontrollably and threatened the cosmos, so Brahma and forty-four other deities pinned him face-down to the earth, each holding a particular limb. When the being surrendered, Brahma blessed him with the name Vastu Purusha and decreed that any structure built on earth must offer worship to him and to the deities holding his limbs. The Vastu Purusha Stotram salutes this surrender-arrangement. (Sources: Wikipedia Vastu Shastra; Astromantra Vastu Purusha; AppliedVastu Mandala.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Vastu Purusha Mandala related to the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vastu Purusha Mandala is a 9x9 (or 8x8) geometric grid of 81 padas onto which the body of Vastu Purusha is mapped diagonally with his head in the northeast (ishana) and his feet in the southwest (nairutya). Each pada is occupied by a specific deity, totalling forty-five inner and outer devatas. The Vastu Purusha Stotram enumerates these directional deities, beginning with Brahma in the central Brahmasthan and proceeding through the eight cardinal lokapalas (Indra-east, Agni-southeast, Yama-south, Nirruti-southwest, Varuna-west, Vayu-northwest, Kubera-north, Ishana-northeast). Recitation of the stotram is therefore a verbal walk through the mandala, invoking each occupant. (Sources: AppliedVastu Mandala; Plusvalueindia Introduction.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vastu Purusha Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most important recitation occasions are bhumi-puja before laying the foundation stone, shilanyas at the cornerstone ceremony, griha-pravesh on the day of moving into a new home, vastu-shanti homa to remedy structural Vastu defects, and the annual Vastu Puja performed on Akshaya Tritiya, Vasant Panchami or the family's chosen anniversary day. The Vishwakarma Vastu Shastra tradition additionally prescribes weekly recitation on Thursdays as the day of Brihaspati, who governs construction-knowledge. The mantra Om Vastu Purushaya Namah is chanted 108 times preceding the full stotram. (Sources: Astroved Vastu Mantras; TemplePurohit Vastu Mantra; Astromantra.)

seoFaqs.question: Which directional deities are named in the eight-direction portion of the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard eight directional deities (ashta-dikpalas) named in the stotram are Indra in the east as lord of authority and rain, Agni in the southeast as lord of fire and the kitchen, Yama in the south as lord of dharma and ancestors, Nirruti in the southwest as lord of stability and storage, Varuna in the west as lord of waters and downloads, Vayu in the northwest as lord of air and movement of guests, Kubera in the north as lord of wealth and treasury, and Ishana (Shiva) in the northeast as lord of pure consciousness and the puja room. Brahma occupies the central Brahmasthan. The stotram salutes each by name and seeks their blessings on the corresponding portion of the home. (Sources: AppliedVastu; Astromantra; Wikipedia Vastu Shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do householders seek by reciting Vastu Purusha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Recitation is performed to secure the deity's permission for any construction or renovation activity, neutralise vastu-dosha arising from defects in plot orientation or room placement, prevent the negative effects of compromises forced by site geometry, attract Lakshmi-flow and prosperity, ensure the health of family members occupying particular zones, and guard against unseen disturbance attributed to the directional deities. Builders and architects in the Vishwakarma tradition recite it before commencing work each day. Many families chant the stotram on the first day of every Hindu month or at the start of major financial years to renew the dwelling's protective charge. (Sources: Vedicrishi Vastu Mantra; TemplePurohit; Astroved.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Vastu Purusha Stotram differ from Vastu Shanti Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Vastu Purusha Stotram is a praise-and-invocation hymn focused on saluting the deity and the forty-five surrounding devatas of the mandala; it can be recited on its own as a daily prayer or as the opening invocation of any Vastu ceremony. Vastu Shanti Stotram, drawn primarily from Skanda Purana and Matsya Purana liturgical material, is a longer remedial prayer recited specifically during the vastu-shanti homa to pacify any disturbances caused to Vastu Purusha by construction work, and to harmonise the building's energy field. The Purusha Stotram invokes; the Shanti Stotram pacifies. Most home rituals recite both in sequence. (Sources: Templepurohit; Astromantra; Vedic Rishi.)
